Introduction
?

A first-order linear system of differential equations with two dependent variables is a system of equations that takes the following standard form: where

  • and are known functions, and

  • and are unknown functions (dependent variables).

For example, is a first-order linear system of ODEs since it can be written in the standard form where

On the other hand, is not a first-order linear system of ODEs since it contains the term

Similar definitions apply to systems with three or more variables can be defined similarly.

Example: Identifying a Linear System of Differential Equations
?

Which of the following systems of differential equations are linear?

EXPLANATION

A first-order linear system of differential equations with two dependent variables is a system of equations that takes the following standard form: where

  • and are known functions, and

  • and are unknown functions (dependent variables).

With this in mind, let's inspect each system:

  • System I is not a first-order linear system of ODEs since it contains the terms and

  • System II is a first-order linear system of ODEs since it can be written in the standard form, where

Therefore, only the second system is linear.

KEY PREREQUISITE
Introduction to First-Order Linear ODEs
Question 1
?

Which of the following are first-order linear systems of ODEs?

  1. {x1′(t)=−x1(t)−7cos⁡(t)x2(t)x2′(t)=−2x1(t)−3x2(t)
  2. {x1′(t)=x12(t)+x2(t)x2′(t)=x1(t)+sin⁡(t)x2(t)
  3. {x1′(t)=x1(t)+ex2(t)x2′(t)=−5x1(t)+3cos⁡(x2(t))
a
I and II only
b
I and III only
c
II and III only
d
None
e
I only
Submit
Question 2
?

Which of the following are first-order linear systems of ODEs?

  1. {x1′(t)=tx12(t)+x2(t)x2′(t)=2x1(t)+13x23(t)
  2. {x1′(t)=3x1(t)+sin⁡(2t)x2′(t)=6x2(t)−1
  3. {x1′(t)=ln⁡(x1(t))+x2(t)x2′(t)=2x1(t)+x1(t)x2(t)+t
a
I only
b
II and III only
c
II only
d
III only
e
I and II only

Homogeneous and Inhomogeneous Systems
?

Recall that a first-order linear system of differential equations with two dependent variables is a system of the form If and (i.e., both and are zero functions), then the system is called homogeneous. Otherwise, if at least one of or is nonzero, the system is inhomogeneous.

In matrix form, the system is homogeneous when and inhomogeneous when

For example:

  • The system below is homogeneous because :

  • The system below is inhomogeneous because of the term :

Example: Identifying Homogeneous and Inhomogeneous Systems
?

Which of the following systems are homogeneous?

EXPLANATION

Recall that a first-order linear system of differential equations with two dependent variables is a system of the form If and (i.e., both and are zero functions), then the system is homogeneous. Otherwise, if at least one of or is not identically zero, the system is inhomogeneous.

With this in mind, let's inspect each system:

  • System I is homogeneous since it can be written in the standard form with and

  • System II is inhomogeneous since it can be written in the standard form with and

  • System III is homogeneous since it can be written in the standard form with and

Therefore, only the first and third systems are homogeneous.

Question 5
?

Which of the following systems are homogeneous?

  1. x′(t)=[−3105]x(t)
  2. x′(t)=[02−20]x(t)+[0−t]
  3. x′(t)=[−1200]x(t)
a
I and III only
b
II and III only
c
I only
d
I and II only
e
None
Submit

?

  1. {x1′(t)=x1(t)−7x2(t)x2′(t)=−2x1(t)−3x2(t)

  3. {x1′(t)=x1(t)+x2(t)+etx2′(t)=−5x1(t)+3x2(t)+2
a
II and III only
b
I and II only
c
None
d
I only
e
I and III only
